upgrade_path: Direct upgrade from 3.6.x or 3.7.x. Users on 3.5.x should first upgrade to 3.6.4 to apply schema migrations.
release_summary: Velocistream 3.8 introduces native Parquet export from streaming pipelines, reworked back-pressure management for high-throughput connectors, and fourteen bug fixes including a critical fix for the Kafka offset commit race condition reported in VST-4921. The deprecated XML serializer has been removed.

added_items.title: Native Parquet export in streaming pipelines
added_items.description: Streaming sinks can now write directly to Parquet format without an intermediate CSV stage. Supports Snappy and ZSTD compression. Reduces storage costs by approximately 40% for columnar-heavy workloads. See `ParquetSinkConfig` in the SDK reference.

added_items.title: Pipeline-level resource quotas
added_items.description: Operators can set per-pipeline CPU and memory limits via the new `resources.limits` configuration block. Exceeding limits triggers graceful back-pressure rather than OOM termination.

added_items.title: OpenTelemetry trace propagation for connectors
added_items.description: All built-in connectors now propagate W3C Trace Context headers end-to-end. Enables distributed tracing across Velocistream pipelines and downstream consumers without custom middleware.

added_items.title: CLI command: vstream pipeline inspect
added_items.description: New CLI command that prints a detailed DAG visualization of a running pipeline, including per-stage throughput, buffer utilization, and current checkpoint offsets.

changed_items.title: Back-pressure algorithm upgraded to adaptive token bucket
changed_items.description: Replaced the fixed-window rate limiter with an adaptive token-bucket algorithm that adjusts based on downstream consumer acknowledgment latency. Improves throughput by 18–25% under sustained load in benchmark testing.

changed_items.title: Default checkpoint interval reduced to 5 seconds
changed_items.description: Previously 30 seconds. Reduces potential data replay on recovery. Configurable via `checkpoint.interval_ms`. Existing configurations are not overwritten on upgrade.

changed_items.title: Kafka connector upgraded to librdkafka 2.5.0
changed_items.description: Brings support for KRaft-mode brokers and cooperative sticky partition assignment. The `partition.assignment.strategy` default changes from `range` to `cooperative-sticky`.

fixed_items.title: Kafka offset commit race condition (VST-4921)
fixed_items.description: Under high partition rebalance frequency, offsets could be committed for revoked partitions, causing duplicate processing on consumer restart. Root cause was a missing lock acquisition in the async commit callback path.

fixed_items.title: Memory leak in windowed aggregation with late arrivals (VST-5003)
fixed_items.description: Tumbling windows with allowed lateness greater than the window size accumulated state entries that were never evicted. Fixed by aligning the eviction watermark with the lateness boundary.

fixed_items.title: S3 sink multipart upload failures on network retry (VST-5112)
fixed_items.description: When an individual part upload was retried after a transient network error, the ETag tracking array contained duplicates, causing the CompleteMultipartUpload call to fail with InvalidPart.

fixed_items.title: Incorrect timestamp parsing for ISO 8601 with microsecond precision (VST-5087)
fixed_items.description: Timestamps with exactly six fractional digits were truncated to milliseconds instead of preserving microsecond precision. Affected the `event_time` extraction in the JSON deserializer.

fixed_items.title: CLI --dry-run flag ignored for delete operations (VST-5140)
fixed_items.description: The `vstream pipeline delete --dry-run` command was executing the actual deletion. Added guard check in the command dispatch layer.

removed_items.title: XML serializer (deprecated since 3.5.0)
removed_items.description: The built-in XML serializer and `XmlSinkConfig` have been removed. Users requiring XML output should migrate to the community-maintained `velocistream-xml-plugin` package, which supports the same configuration schema.

removed_items.title: Legacy v1 checkpoint format support
removed_items.description: Checkpoint files in the v1 binary format (used prior to 3.0.0) are no longer readable. Run `vstream checkpoint migrate` on any v1 checkpoints before upgrading.

breaking_changes:
1. The Kafka connector default partition assignment strategy is now `cooperative-sticky`. Pipelines relying on `range` assignment must explicitly set `partition.assignment.strategy: range` in their connector configuration.
2. The XML serializer has been removed. Replace `type: xml` sinks with the `velocistream-xml-plugin` connector.
3. Checkpoint v1 format is no longer supported. Migrate using `vstream checkpoint migrate --format v2` before upgrading.
known_issues:
- Parquet export does not yet support nested map types with nullable values. A fix is tracked in VST-5198 and expected in 3.8.1.
- The resource quota enforcement for GPU-accelerated stages is not yet implemented; CPU and memory limits work correctly.
contributors: Maren Solberg, Tomás Arévalo, Priya Chandrasekar, Kofi Mensah, and the Velocistream open-source community. Special thanks to Rúnar Ólafsson for the initial Parquet sink implementation and Aya Nakamura for extensive testing of the back-pressure algorithm under production-scale loads.
